methods.description: The adsorption experiments were conducted to determine the maximum toluene removal using diatomite at natural pH, which is suitable for industrial purposes. The procedure involves mixing diatomite with toluene solutions, agitating the mixture, and measuring the changes in absorbance over time to calculate the amount of toluene adsorbed.

steps.description: Mix various amounts of diatomite (0.03 – 0.12 g) in 100 mL of toluene solutions with varying concentrations (100-300 mg/L) at natural pH (pH = 6).
steps.searchable_summary: mix diatomite with toluene solutions at natural pH
outputs: mixture of diatomite and toluene solution

steps.description: Conduct adsorption experiments at optimum amount of diatomite (0.1 g) at pH 6, an agitation speed of 200 rpm and temperature 25 ± 1°C for 1 h to attain equilibrium conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct adsorption with optimal diatomite amount and agitation

steps.description: Determine changes of absorbance at certain time intervals (5, 10, 30, 60 and 90 min) during the adsorption process.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance changes at specified intervals

steps.description: Centrifuge the toluene solutions for 10 min in a Hettich EBA20 centrifuge at 4000 rpm to separate the sorbent from the solution.
steps.searchable_summary: centrifuge toluene solutions to separate sorbent

steps.description: Calculate the amount of toluene adsorbed by the adsorbent at equilibrium, q e (mg/g), using the equation: q e = (C0 - Ce) * V / W.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate adsorbed toluene using equilibrium equation
